precision_policy: ADR-007 gated-fp32 ship mode: theta/u/v/qv/qke fp32; mu/p/ph/w + acoustic/pressure accumulators fp64.
headline: d03 1km in gated-fp32 (the operational ship mode) goes NONFINITE after forecast hour 1. The single offending field is qke (MYNN turbulent kinetic energy), fp32, 3036 nonfinite cells; every other prognostic field stays finite. The 24h all-timesteps-in-margins gate is therefore NOT met in gated-fp32.

qke.note: MYNN TKE. fp32 overflow at 1km (steep Tenerife terrain + dt=3s -> large TKE production/dissipation gradients exceed fp32 dynamic range/precision).

root_cause: qke is FP32_GATED in src/gpuwrf/contracts/precision.py:112. At 1km the MYNN TKE budget loses too much precision / overflows in fp32. qke is NOT in the dycore mass/pressure/acoustic path, so it is not protected by the fp64-locked accumulators that keep d02 stable.
actionable_fix_OUT_OF_BURST_SCOPE: Promote qke (and likely the MYNN length-scale intermediates) to FP64 in the gated precision matrix for the 1km path. qke is a diagnostic-style turbulence field outside the conserved mass/pressure path, so promoting it preserves the gated-fp32 invariants and the d02 speedup (qke is a small field). This is a precision-contract change to precision.py and therefore needs its own bounded sprint + review per the operating rules; it was NOT done in this validation burst.

fp64_reference_state.verdict: D03_FINITE (full fp64) confirmed previously on trunk
fp64_reference_state.fp64_throughput_caveat: FP64 is ~1:64-throttled on the RTX 5090; the d03 fp64 increment is ~16 min per 0.3h, so a full 24h fp64 d03 validation cannot complete inside this burst window. A 24h fp64 d03 run would take many hours and is the slow path the gated-fp32 mode exists to replace.
fp64_reference_state.implication: d03 1km is finite in fp64 but impractically slow; gated-fp32 is fast but NaNs on qke. Neither practical mode closes the 24h 1km gate today -> the qke-fp64 promotion is the path that makes the 1km gate achievable at ship speed.

wall_clock.observed_compile_phases: two ~4-min XLA compiles (no-radiation hour graph + radiation-active hour graph) before the blow-up surfaced; the hour-1 no-radiation graph compiled in ~4m20s (XLA slow-compile alarm logged).

validation_fields_obtained: NONE (forecast NaN'd at hour 1 before any wrfout was written)
honesty_note: This is a genuine OPEN gate, reported as FAIL not green. The d03-1km gate with the new faithful physics is NOT validated in the ship (gated-fp32) precision. The blocker is precisely isolated (qke fp32) and the fix is identified and low-risk, but implementing/validating it is a follow-up sprint, not this burst.
